message:
perf(storage): cache message batch INSERT SQL + return &str from role_str_ref

Two micro-allocations on the hot conversation-merge path were showing
up in profiles when the merger lands hundreds of conversations against
a warm DB.

(1) franken_batch_insert_new_messages was rebuilding the multi-row
    INSERT SQL string on every call: a Vec<String> of 100 placeholder
    triplets, an enumerate map, a join, and a final format! producing
    a fresh String. The SQL only depends on chunk.len() (which ranges
    over 2..=100), so all 99 possible texts can be generated once and
    then handed back as &'static str.

    - Add MESSAGE_BATCH_INSERT_SQL: OnceLock<Vec<String>> indexed by
      row count, with row count 0 and 1 left empty (the single-row
      case still goes through franken_insert_new_message).
    - cached_message_batch_insert_sql(rows) returns the &'static str
      for the requested row count via OnceLock::get_or_init, falling
      back through .get() so a future regression that calls with
      rows > 100 panics with a clear message rather than silently
      producing malformed SQL.
    - Replace the inline placeholder/format building with a single
      cached_message_batch_insert_sql(chunk.len()) lookup.

(2) role_str(&MessageRole) was returning String for every call, even
    though four of the five variants are static literals. The fifth
    (Other) is an owned String that would need to be cloned to fit a
    &str return shape. Provide both:

    - role_str_ref(&MessageRole) -> &str returns the underlying
      static slice for User/Agent/Tool/System and the Other(v) inner
      String's slice — zero allocation on the common path.
    - role_str(&MessageRole) -> String now wraps role_str_ref().to_owned()
      so existing callers that need an owned string keep working.
    - Three hot call sites (franken_insert_new_message,
      franken_batch_insert_new_messages, and the analytics row build
      in upsert_conversation_profiled) switch to role_str_ref. The
      session-event ParamValue paths that need an owned String for
      lifetime reasons keep calling role_str() (now via .to_string()).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
